intakeRaw.currentRoleCompany: Country sales and operations lead at a mid-size industrial distributor
intakeRaw.mainDecision: Single-path diagnosis: determine whether the post-retirement 12–18 month structure is financially and operationally sustainable if the retirement package is accepted.
intakeRaw.optionsUnderConsideration: Single-path assessment of retirement-package transition readiness and survivability.
intakeRaw.forcedChoiceToday: Package timeline creates near-term pressure; decision quality depends on whether transition structure is fully defensible.
topPriorities: Monthly burn control and liquidity discipline
Maintaining employability while not forcing a weak early move
intakeRaw.nonNegotiable: Do not trigger retirement unless household runway and transition execution capacity are both structurally viable.
intakeRaw.biggestRisks: Underestimating job-search duration and overestimating short-term conversion probability after exit.
intakeRaw.marketDemandReason: Market has openings but hiring selectivity and compensation pressure make timing-sensitive re-entry risk material.
intakeRaw.companyStability: Somewhat stable, but with noticeable changes or uncertainty
intakeRaw.restructuringHistory: Yes – a few changes, but mostly stable
intakeRaw.sponsorSupport: No – I don’t have a clear sponsor yet
intakeRaw.commitmentStyle: I prefer small, reversible steps and testing before fully committing.
intakeRaw.energizingWorkStyle: Structured problem solving with measurable operating impact and clear decision rights.
intakeRaw.stayScenario12to18m: If exit is delayed, short-term income remains intact but transition window may narrow and package quality may decline.
intakeRaw.planBStrength: I have almost no safety net if this fails.
intakeRaw.mustAnswerQuestion: Is the 12–18 month post-exit structure truly survivable without forced low-quality re-entry?
